landmark_label: NW corner grate (v0.7 candidate; survey elev 3.80 NAVD88 same as NE)

landmark_label: Pavement-low pocket near SE grate (NOT a landmark in v0.6; pavement ~3.48-3.52 NAVD88)
observed_qualitative: persistent puddle in pocket while broader road has receded — visual confirmation of post-overflow retention
notes: Photo 21 EXIF. 16 min after NOAA peak. Pocket is downstream of SE grate; water can only reach it after grate overflows; it retains water during ebb because gravity can't push water back uphill into the grate.

landmark_label: Pavement-low pocket near SE grate (pavement ~3.48-3.52 NAVD88)
observed_qualitative: water clearly present in pocket, also wet across crosswalk markings near SE grate — residual from previous evening's 22:12 peak
notes: Photo 1 EXIF (2026-05-19 11:51:50 UTC = 07:51:50 EDT). ~9.7h after prior evening's 22:12 peak. SH 2.85 ft MLLW (= 0.03 NAVD88 at gauge), 3.17 ft below grate_SE threshold (SH 6.02 MLLW). Pocket water is purely residual; current bay is rising AM tide but nowhere near grate. Confirms ≥9.7h retention with no rain and only early sun.

landmark_label: Pavement-low pocket near SE grate (pavement ~3.48-3.52 NAVD88)
observed_qualitative: water present in pocket; SE grate visible in same frame, clearly sub-flush (current bay still well below grate top) — textbook disambiguation example
notes: Photo 2 EXIF (2026-05-19 12:09:12 UTC = 08:09:12 EDT). ~9.95h after prior evening's 22:12 peak. SH 3.22 ft MLLW (= 0.40 NAVD88 at gauge), 2.80 ft below grate_SE threshold. Photo shows pocket water AND SE grate top in the same frame — grate clearly dry, current tide nowhere near overflow. Exemplifies the spot-check disambiguation rule (read grate slot to determine if pocket water is current vs residual).

landmark_label: Pavement-low pocket near SE grate (pavement ~3.48-3.52 NAVD88)
observed_qualitative: water STILL present in pocket (visual, drive-by) — 'healthy amount' per user — retention ≥11.4 hours from prior evening's 22:12 peak
notes: Visual drive-by observation (no photo). NOAA SH 4.81 ft MLLW at 09:33 — still 1.21 ft below grate_SE threshold, so pocket water remains purely residual. User expects persistence beyond 09:33 departure. Combined with the two earlier morning photos, this confirms pocket retention ≥11.4h overnight from a single evening tidal overflow with no rain and modest solar drying.

landmark_label: Lowest sentinel grate (SE proximal; survey elev 3.60 NAVD88)
observed_qualitative: bone dry well above the grate top — no surface water anywhere; ~15 min post-AM-peak
notes: Photo expected in assets/observations/2026-05-30/grate-SE-proximal/. AM peak SH 4.592 at 08:06 ET (vs predicted 4.355 at 07:53; surge +0.24). v0.6 model with observed SH agrees: water 2.14 NAVD88 → 1.46 ft below grate top → 0 depth predicted, 0 observed. Final logged forecast made T-4.8h over-predicted SH (5.014 via surge-persistence) but outcome was dry either way.

landmark_label: Lowest sentinel grate (SE proximal; survey elev 3.60 NAVD88)
observed_qualitative: drive-by 1-1.5h post-PM-peak; water visible over the grate slots — peak overtopping confirmed (exact peak depth not measured)
notes: Qualitative only, no photo. PM peak SH 6.538 ft MLLW at 20:36 ET (vs predicted 5.491 at 20:07; surge +1.05, time delay +29 min). At observation time SH was still 6.40 (interpolated). Final logged forecast at T-4.57h (corrected — earlier draft said T-11.6h was last; that was a grep error) under-predicted SH by 0.69 ft (5.844 vs 6.538) and called regime 'dry'; should have been at least 'street'. With observed SH peak fed into v0.6 model: water 4.118 NAVD88, grate_SE peak depth ~6.2 inches. Event consistent with both +0.40 and ~0 enhancement (both predict over-SE/SW, under-curb) — does NOT discriminate.

landmark_label: SW distal grate (v0.7 candidate; ~3.55-3.58 NAVD88 inferred)
observed_qualitative: NOT directly observed; inferred overtopped at peak from SE-grate observation — SW is ~0.5 inches lower than SE so if SE overtopped, SW did too
notes: Inference, not direct observation. Useful as a model-calibration constraint: peak SH 6.538 was sufficient to overtop both grate_SE (3.60) and grate_SW (~3.55-3.58 NAVD88).

landmark_label: Pavement-low pocket near SE grate (pavement ~3.48-3.52 NAVD88)
observed_qualitative: water present in pocket on drive-by — bay still receding from peak (1-1.5h post); pocket is both current AND retention here, not pure residual
notes: Qualitative only, no photo. PM peak SH 6.538 at 20:36 ET; at observation time SH still 6.40 (well above grate_SE 3.60 even after subtracting datum). Another data point for SE-grate overtopping events leaving pocket water that persists post-peak.
